channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Bolivian miners clash with police demanding president resign
item.description: Bolivian miners clash with police demanding that President Paz resign.

item.title: Helicopters douse flames threatening hillside homes in California
item.description: Video shows helicopters dumping water on a fast-moving wildfire in southern California’s Simi Valley.

item.title: How Philadelphia’s Democratic primary tests the bounds of US progressivism
item.description: The race for Pennsylvania's third congressional district reveals divisions between Democrats closely aligned on policy.

item.title: Trump’s repeated ultimatums betray his lack of leverage over Iran
item.description: Foad Izadi says US President Trump's repeated threats of renewed full-scale war are a sign of weakness.

item.title: WHO responding to ‘complex, difficult’ Ebola outbreak in DR Congo
item.description: The World Health Organization is getting health experts and supplies to DR Congo to battle the Ebola outbreak.

item.title: Russia-China ties ‘stabilising’ force in world, Putin says before Xi talks
item.description: Russia's Vladimir Putin is travelling to China for second meeting with Chinese leader Xi Jinping in less than a year.

item.title: Two teen gunmen kill three at San Diego mosque in suspected hate crime
item.description: Two teen gunmen kill three at San Diego mosque in suspected hate crime.

item.title: Iran war live: Trump says Iran attack postponed at request of Gulf allies
item.description: Al Jazeera's correspondent in Tehran says Iran's leaders are 'projecting defiance' and rejecting 'pressure' from the US.

item.title: US suspends joint defence effort with Canada dating back to World War II
item.description: The Trump administration has frequently accused US allies of failing to live up to mutual defence obligations.

item.title: A packed race for governor: What to know about Oregon’s primary elections
item.description: The primaries should winnow down the nearly two dozen candidates seeking to challenge incumbent Governor Tina Kotek.

item.title: Brazil call up Neymar to World Cup 2026 squad
item.description: Brazil's all-time leading scorer Neymar called up for World Cup 2026 as Carlo Ancelotti turns to forward for first time.

item.title: A redistricting re-do? What to know about Alabama’s primary elections
item.description: Voters may have to return to the polls in August as state seeks to implement a previously blocked redistricting push.

item.title: Is the US trying to force regime change in Cuba?
item.description: As its blockade causes a fuel crisis in Cuba, the US is offering aid but only if it can dictate Cuba's future.

item.title: Guardiola on verge of Manchester City exit at end of Premier League season
item.description: Manchester City manager Pep Guardiola is understood to be stepping down at end of season after 17 years at EPL club.

item.title: Venezuelan protesters clash with police at rally for political prisoners
item.description: Protesters in Venezuela have clashed with police at a rally for political prisoners.

item.title: Clashes as Morales-allied protesters march on Bolivian capital
item.description: South American country suffers huge protests as economic crisis fuels unrest against President Paz's government.

item.title: Protest in Greece after Israeli forces storm Gaza aid flotilla
item.description: Demonstrators gathered outside Greece’s foreign ministry in Athens.

item.title: Trump pauses possible Iran strike after Gulf intervention
item.description: US President Donald Trump has announced that he is delaying a planned attack on Iran.

item.title: Arsenal have one hand on Premier League title after beating Burnley
item.description: Arsenal are one win away from their first Premier League title since 2004 following a 1-0 win against Burnley.

item.title: ‘Not for sale’: Greenland premier tells US envoy
item.description: Prime Minister Jens-Frederik Nielsen says while the meeting was "constructive" nothing "had changed" on the US position

item.title: Iraq launches desert sweep after reports of secret Israeli bases
item.description: Iraqi security forces have launched sweeping operations in the country’s western desert.

item.title: Indian court declares another historical mosque a temple
item.description: An Indian High Court has ruled that the medieval Kamal Maula mosque is a temple originally dedicated to a Hindu goddess.

item.title: Croatian president rejects new Israeli ambassador
item.description: President Zoran Milanovic says Israel broke an 'unwritten rule' by announcing its ambassador before approval.

item.title: US drops fraud charges after billionaire Adani pledges $10bn investment
item.description: Adani was accused of bribing Indian officials and misleading US investors to secure a solar power plant project in India.

item.title: Will Cuba be forced to accept the US president’s demands?
item.description: Cuba struggles to keep the lights on, as Washington ramps up economic pressure and military threats.
